hook.text: A concussion doesn't bruise the brain. It disrupts the brain's chemical and electrical signaling.

buildup.text: Rapid acceleration shears brain cell connections and triggers a cascade of metabolic dysfunction.

discovery.text: Potassium floods out of cells and calcium floods in, creating an energy crisis in the brain.

twist.text: CT scans and MRIs usually look normal after a concussion—the damage is biochemical, not structural.

climax.text: The brain needs metabolic rest to restore ion balance—which is why cognitive rest helps recovery.

punchline.text: Invisible on scans. Real in symptoms. Chemical storms, not bruises.

quiz.question: Why do concussions usually not show on CT scans?
options: The damage is biochemical and metabolic, not structural
CT scans can't image the brain
